title: 10 best herbal tinctures for periodontal disease

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Great burnet tinctures helps with periodontal disease because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation. The tincture's antioxidants also help to combat the bacterial infection that causes periodontitis by neutralizing free radicals and promoting a healthy environment for the gums. Additionally, great burnet's antimicrobial properties inhibit the growth of bacteria, further supporting the body's natural defense against infection and promoting a healthy oral microbiome.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Great Burnet tinctures contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with periodontal disease.

Antimicrobial properties: The tannins and flavonoids in Great Burnet tinctures have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against various oral pathogens, including bacteria that cause periodontitis, helping to prevent infection and promote healing.

Wound-healing properties: Great Burnet tinctures contain compounds that stimulate collagen synthesis and improve blood flow, which can aid in the repair of damaged tissue and accelerate wound healing in cases of periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are valued for their ability to reduce inflammation and combat infection, making them an effective treatment for periodontal disease.
Barks: The barks are used due to their high concentration of flavonoids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe gum tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality great burnet roots and leaves from a clean environment.
Wash the harvested plant material with filtered water and gentle agitation.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator at 60°f for 12 hours.
Combine 20 grams of dried great burnet root and leaf mixture with 100 proof vodka in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks, shaking daily, then strain and filter the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Danshen tinctures helps with periodontal disease because they contain salvanolic acid, a potent antioxidant that has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body. By targeting the underlying causes of periodontal disease, such as bacterial biofilms and gum tissue damage, Danshen tinctures may help to prevent further progression of the disease. Additionally, the anticoagulant properties of Danshen may also help to reduce bleeding and promote healing in affected areas, making it a promising natural treatment option for periodontitis sufferers.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Danshen tincture's anti-inflammatory effect helps to reduce swelling, pain, and inflammation associated with periodontal disease, promoting a healthier gum environment.

Antimicrobial property: The antimicrobial properties of Danshen tincture inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis, reducing the severity of the condition.

Oxidant and free radical scavenging activity: Danshen tincture's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to periodontal tissue damage and bone loss.
remedy_parts: Roots: Danshen roots have been traditionally used to treat oral infections and inflammation, making them a popular choice for creating medicinal tinctures for periodontal disease.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of danshen contain bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and prevent bone loss associated with periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia miltiorrhiza roots and rinse them thoroughly with cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and measure out 100g of dried root material to ensure optimal extraction.
Combine the chopped root material with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar and let it steep for 14 days.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids after transferring the tincture to a dark glass bottle.
Label the bottle with the date and ingredients and store it in a cool dry place for up to two years before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with periodontal disease because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which combat the underlying bacteria that cause gum inflammation and infection. The tincture's antioxidant-rich compounds also help reduce oxidative stress, promoting healthy gum tissue and bone regeneration. Additionally, Maidenhair tree's anti-inflammatory properties soothe gum swelling and pain, while its antibacterial activities prevent further damage to teeth and surrounding tissues. By addressing the root causes of periodontal disease, Maidenhair tree tinctures offer a natural and effective solution for maintaining optimal oral health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in gum tissues, thereby mitigating periodontal disease progression.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Maidenhair Tree tincture inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which contributes to the reduction of gingivitis and periodontitis symptoms.

Antimicrobial activity: Ginkgo biloba's antimicrobial compounds exhibit broad-spectrum activity against various bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can contribute to periodontal disease, helping to prevent secondary infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in treating periodontal disease by reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial growth.

Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, helping to combat periodontitis by reducing oxidative stress and inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of ginkgo biloba leaves and stems, wash them gently in cold water and dry thoroughly.
Chop the dried ginkgo biloba into small pieces and combine with 500ml of high-proof vodka or everclear in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction, storing it in a dark place at room temperature.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Store the finished tincture in a cool, dark place for up to 2 years, using 10-15 drops three times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with periodontal disease because it has potent antibacterial properties that target the bacteria responsible for causing gum inflammation and infection. The tannins present in lemon balm also help to reduce swelling and ease pain, allowing for a healthier and more comfortable mouth. Additionally, lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds can help to reduce inflammation in the gums and surrounding tissues, promoting healing and preventing further damage.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Lemon balm's antibacterial compounds, such as citral and linalool, can help combat harmful bacteria in the mouth that contribute to periodontal disease.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, may help reduce inflammation in the gums and surrounding tissues, which is a common symptom of periodontal disease.
Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm's antioxidant compounds can help protect against oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gum disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lemon balm plant are rich in bioactive compounds that have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective against gum inflammation and bacteria associated with periodontal disease.
Flowers: Lemon balm flowers contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been demonstrated to reduce plaque formation, prevent gingivitis, and soothe oral mucosa irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and flowers for the herbal material.
Clean and dry the harvested material thoroughly to remove impurities.
Chop the dried herb into small pieces and mix with high-proof vodka in a 1:5 ratio by weight.
Store the mixture in an airtight glass container and allow it to macerate for 6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, and transfer it to dark glass bottles for storage and use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Germander tinctures helps with periodontal disease because it possesses antibacterial properties that target the root causes of this condition. The tincture's active compounds, such as geraniol and limonene, have been shown to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth, including Porphyromonas gingivalis and Aggregatibacter actinomycetemcomitans, which are commonly associated with periodontal disease. Additionally, herbal germander tinctures may also help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gums, ultimately alleviating symptoms of this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Germander's antibacterial properties help combat the bacteria responsible for periodontal disease, reducing inflammation and preventing further damage to the gums and bone.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Germander's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with periodontitis, promoting healing and comfort in affected areas.

Antioxidant activity: Germander's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate periodontal disease and slow down the healing process.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Germander contain bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote healing, making them effective in treating periodontal diseases.
Barks: The barks of Germander have been traditionally used to treat gum infections and inflammatory conditions, making them a valuable part for medicinal tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried teucrium chamaedrys leaves and stems in amounts of at least 20% of the total volume.
Combine the harvested material with 60-70% vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass container, ensuring the herb is completely submerged.
Steep the mixture for six to eight weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the container every few days, allowing for efficient extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark amber glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store in a cool dark place for up to five years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ironwort tinctures helps with periodontal disease because of its powerful antimicrobial properties that target the bacteria responsible for gum inflammation. The tincture's unique combination of flavonoids and phenolic acids has been shown to reduce plaque formation, prevent tooth decay, and even eliminate existing biofilms. Additionally, ironwort's anti-inflammatory compounds help alleviate swollen gums and reduce pain associated with periodontal disease. As a natural alternative to conventional treatments, herbal ironwort tinctures offer a promising solution for maintaining healthy teeth and gums.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Ironwort tincture has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis and Fusobacterium nucleatum.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in ironwort tincture have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with periodontitis.

Antioxidant properties: Ironwort tincture contains antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals and oxidative stress, which can contribute to tissue damage and inflammation in the gums and periodontal tissues.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in treating gum inflammation and combating bacteria associated with periodontal disease.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenoids, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of oral pathogens and reduce inflammation in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sideritis scardica leaves and flowers at peak potency during full moon.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7-10 days.
Combine 200g of dried sideritis scardica with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place and allow it to macerate for 14-21 days shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose tinctures helps with periodontal disease because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the gums. The herb's antimicrobial compounds also work to combat bacterial growth, promoting a healthy balance of oral flora and preventing further infection. Additionally, beach rose has been shown to stimulate collagen production, strengthening tooth enamel and gum tissue, making it an effective natural remedy for treating periodontal disease and maintaining overall oral health.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in beach rose tincture can reduce swelling and pain associated with periodontal disease, promoting healing and reducing discomfort.
Wound-healing properties: Beach rose tincture's wound-healing compounds can stimulate the body's natural repair processes, helping to regenerate gum tissue and promote healthy oral mucosa.

Barks: The barks of the beach rose plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums, promoting healing and reducing symptoms of periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality rosa rugosa flowers in mid-summer for optimal bioactive compounds.
Dry the fresh flowers using a food dehydrator or air circulation method at 40°c for 3-4 hours.
Combine 1 part dried rosa rugosa flowers with 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar and store in a dark cupboard.
Steep the mixture for 6 weeks, shaking the jar every other day, before straining the liquid through cheesecloth into a measuring cup.
Transfer the clear tincture to small amber glass bottles and label with the date, ingredients, and dosage instructions of 20-30 drops three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with periodontal disease because of its natural antibacterial properties, which effectively combat the bacteria that cause gum inflammation and infection. The menthol content in peppermint also has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, reducing pain and swelling associated with periodontitis. Additionally, peppermint's antiseptic properties help to reduce plaque buildup on teeth and gums, promoting healthy oral tissues and preventing further deterioration of the disease. By using herbal peppermint tinctures, individuals can experience relief from symptoms and potentially reverse the progression of periodontal disease.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial Properties: Peppermint's antibacterial properties can help combat the bacteria that cause periodontal disease, reducing inflammation and preventing further damage to gum tissue.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The menthol in peppermint tincture has anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce swelling and pain associated with periodontal disease, making it easier to manage symptoms.

Antioxidant Properties: Peppermint's antioxidant properties can help protect the gums and surrounding tissues from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthier oral environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help combat bacteria and reduce swelling associated with periodontal disease.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, a thick underground stem, contains menthol and other compounds that help to reduce pain and inflammation in the gums and mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality mentha x piperita leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for maximum potency.
Clean and dry the freshly harvested leaves then chop them into small pieces for efficient extraction.
Combine 1 part chopped peppermint leaves with 4 parts vodka in a clean glass jar and let it sit for 6 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container discarding solids after 2 hours of maceration.
Transfer the tincture to dark amber glass bottles fill to top and label with contents date and dosage instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Veldt grape tinctures helps with periodontal disease because it contains potent antioxidants and antimicrobial compounds that combat the bacteria responsible for causing inflammation and tissue damage. The tannins and flavonoids present in veldt grape tincture have been shown to inhibit the growth of pathogens, reducing plaque formation and gingivitis. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain associated with periodontal disease, promoting healthy gums and teeth. Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and polyphenols, can reduce swelling and pain associated with periodontitis by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and signaling pathways.

Antimicrobial Properties: Herbal Veldt Grape Tincture contains antimicrobial compounds that can help combat pathogenic bacteria, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis, which are commonly found in periodontal pockets and contribute to the progression of disease.

Rhyzomes: Containing anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, the rhyzomes reduce swelling and oxidative stress caused by periodontitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cissus quadrangularis leaves and stems, rinse thoroughly with distilled water, and dry for two hours in the shade.
Combine one part dried cissus quadrangularis material with four parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring complete submersion.
Store the mixture in a cool, dark place for eight weeks, shaking the jar twice daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding solids and retaining 30ml of the resulting tincture.
Transfer the remaining tincture to smaller dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, labeling each with preparation date and contents, and store in the refrigerator for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with periodontal disease because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which combat the bacteria that cause gum inflammation and infection. The tannins present in fennel also help to reduce swelling and ease pain associated with periodontitis. Additionally, fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to prevent bone loss and promote healthy gum attachment, ultimately reducing the risk of tooth loss and improving overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Fennel tincture's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and carvone, help to inhibit the growth of bacteria associated with periodontal disease, reducing inflammation and preventing further infection.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and saponins, reduce swelling and pain in the gums and surrounding tissues, making it an effective natural remedy for managing periodontal disease symptoms.

Seeds: Fennel seeds contain a compound called anethole, which has antibacterial properties that help combat the bacteria causing periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh fennel leaves and flowers from a reputable source in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Chop 4 cups of fresh fennel foliage into small pieces using scissors or a food processor to ensure maximum surface area exposure.
Combine chopped fennel with 80 proof vodka as a solvent at a 1:2 ratio by weight in a clean glass jar to extract bioactive compounds.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 6 weeks with regular shaking every other day to allow for optimal extraction and maturation of tincture.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding solids and use as directed by a healthcare professional within one year of preparation date.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry tinctures helps with periodontal disease because they contain powerful antioxidants and polyphenols that combat inflammation in the gums. The antimicrobial properties of blueberries also inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to gum disease, reducing plaque and gingivitis. Additionally, blueberry's ability to improve blood flow and reduce oxidative stress helps to promote healthy gum tissue and bone density, ultimately reducing the severity of periodontal disease. remedy_properties: Anthocyanins: Blueberries are rich in anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to periodontal disease by damaging gum tissue and bone.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in blueberry tinctures have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with periodontal disease, while also preventing bacterial growth and promoting healthy gum attachment.

Manganese: Blueberries are a rich source of manganese, an essential mineral that plays a crucial role in bone health and collagen synthesis, which can help strengthen gums and bones affected by periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, the roots help reduce inflammation and prevent further damage to gum tissue.
Barks: Containing salicin and other anti-inflammatory compounds, the barks have been traditionally used to treat gum inflammation and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh vaccinium myrtillus berries in the morning after dew has dried ensuring optimal potency and quality.
Weigh 200 grams of freshly harvested vaccinium myrtillus berries and combine them with 500 milliliters of organic vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the berry-vodka mixture for four weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction of bioactive compounds and phytochemicals.
After four weeks, strain the mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid tincture.
Store the final vaccinium myrtillus tincture in dark-tinted glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, allowing it to mature for an additional six months before use in treating periodontal disease.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with periodontal disease because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The tannins and flavonoids present in dandelion root extract have been shown to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis, responsible for periodontitis. Anti-inflammatory compounds: The taraxasterol and other polyacetylenes present in dandelion tinctures have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling, pain, and bleeding associated with periodontal disease.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh taraxacum officinale roots in the early morning and wash them gently with clean water.
Chop the roots into small pieces and mix them with 500 milliliters of vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
After the preparation time has passed, filter the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Transfer the clear liquid tincture to dark glass bottles and store it in a cool place for up to 2 years, taking 30-40 drops per day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tree turmeric tinctures helps with periodontal disease because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The curcuminoids present in turmeric have been shown to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can contribute to the development and progression of periodontal disease. Additionally, turmeric's ability to stimulate collagen production and improve blood flow to the gums helps to promote a healthy oral environment and prevent further damage. remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Turmeric tincture's primary active compound, curcuminoids, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation in the gums and prevent bone loss associated with periodontal disease.

Cytokine modulation: Curcuminoids in turmeric tincture can modulate cytokines, which are signaling molecules that promote inflammation, thereby helping to reduce the severity of gum inflammation and tissue destruction.

Antibacterial properties: Turmeric's essential oil contains compounds like curcumin and other terpenes that have been shown to exhibit antibacterial activity against bacteria such as Porphyromonas gingivalis, a primary culprit in periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizome of turmeric contains curcumin, a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce inflammation in the gums and prevent bone loss associated with periodontal disease.

Roots: Turmeric roots have been traditionally used to treat oral infections and gum diseases due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce plaque, tartar, and bleeding in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried berberis aristata roots and rhizomes for optimal potency.
Cleanly wash and chop the berberis aristata plant material into small pieces for efficient extraction.
Combine one part chopped berberis aristata with three parts high-proof ethanol in a clean glass jar for proper solubility.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow for thorough maceration and solvent penetration.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and label for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen tinctures helps with periodontal disease because it has potent antibacterial properties that target the underlying causes of gum inflammation. The menthol in wintergreen tincture has natural anti-inflammatory effects, reducing swelling and pain associated with periodontitis. remedy_recipe: Harvest gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems, ensuring fresh material with no signs of mold or mildew.
Dry the harvested material using air circulation or desiccation at low temperatures for 24-48 hours.
Grind dried gaultheria procumbens into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, then sift through a 100-mesh screen.
Combine the powdered gaultheria procumbens with 80% vodka or ethanol in a ratio of 1:5 by weight, allowing the mixture to steep for 14-21 days.
Strain the tincture using cheesecloth or a coffee filter, then transfer it to dark glass bottles and store at room temperature for up to 2 years.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for periodontal disease are concentrated liquid extracts made from plant-based ingredients, which have been traditionally used to promote oral health and alleviate symptoms associated with gum disease. These tinctures help by reducing inflammation, fighting bacteria, and improving the overall health of gums and teeth. For example, tinctures made from goldenseal and tea tree oil exhibit antimicrobial properties that combat periodontal pathogens, while licorice root and chamomile soothe irritated gums. By using these herbal remedies, individuals can alleviate bleeding gums, bad breath, and loose teeth, ultimately improving their overall quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat periodontal disease with herbal tinctures is by combining a comprehensive approach that includes regular brushing and flossing, professional dental cleanings, and targeted tincture therapy. Additionally, incorporating adaptogenic herbs like Ashwagandha or Ginkgo Biloba can help alleviate stress and promote overall oral health.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to periodontal disease that are treated with herbal tinctures are gum recession, gingivitis, and pyorrhea.
